task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenToonz application is launched.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct scene file is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scene 'dwanko_run.tnz' is loaded, which can be verified by looking at the application window title bar or the scene browser.

task_completion.description: Verify that the character animation has been horizontally flipped.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The character in the main canvas/viewer is visually mirrored, facing and running towards the left side of the screen (Right-to-Left direction).

task_completion.description: Verify that the Output Settings dialog is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' dialog window is open within the OpenToonz interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the output path and format are correctly configured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Output Settings dialog, the 'Save In' path is set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/flipped_walkcycle/' (or the equivalent folder structure) and the file format dropdown is set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the animation is rendered as a frame sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress window/bar is shown in OpenToonz, or a file manager window shows the target directory populated with multiple PNG frame files.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the OpenToonz GUI to flip and render the animation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with OpenToonz menus, schematic, or output settings. The agent should not be using terminal commands (like ImageMagick or ffmpeg) to flip or convert the images.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually rendered the files from OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: The render progress bar in OpenToonz should be visible at some point, or the output settings must be explicitly configured in the GUI before the files appear in the file manager.

integrity.description: Verify the agent is using the actual OpenToonz application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard OpenToonz interface (viewer, xsheet/timeline, toolbars, FX schematic) is clearly visible and interacted with.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz' which contains a 72-frame 'run' cycle. The character must be visually mirrored to face 'left' (Right-to-Left direction). The output must be a PNG sequence saved exactly to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/flipped_walkcycle/'. The task requires at least 24 frames to be rendered.
